item.title: Clinical benefit of faecal microbiota transplantation administered via a single retention enema as an adjunctive treatment in dogs with chronic enteropathy: a randomised controlled trial.

item.title: Fufang Tongye Shaoshang You repairs ulcerative colitis in mice caused by DSS based on the balanced strategy of "inflammation inhibition-microbiota regulation-mucosa protection".
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a complex inflammatory bowel disorder characterized by immune dysregulation, intestinal barrier dysfunction, and intestinal microbiota imbalance. Current therapies remain...

item.title: Difficile to treat: advanced management strategies in difficult to treat Clostridioides difficile infections.
item.description: Expert review of anti-infective therapy - Review - Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) remains a leading cause of healthcare-associated infectious diarrhea, with a major burden driven by recurrences and severe or even fulminant disease in vuln...

item.title: Amelioration of colitis through restored gut ecology using Christensenella intestinihominis AF73-05CM02 as a probiotic in mice.
item.description: Communications biology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is closely linked to gut microbial imbalance marked by loss of beneficial microbes and overgrowth of pa...

item.title: Clinical response and risk factors of fecal microbiota transplantation in children: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: European journal of pediatrics - Meta-analysis - The objective of this study is to investigate the clinical response and incidence of adverse events (AEs) following fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) in children, across various diseases, popu...

item.title: Peptidomics: A New Dimension in Microbiome Research.
item.description: Protein and peptide letters - The human gut microbiome is now recognised as a major determinant of health, with roles extending beyond digestion to influence neurodegeneration, metabolism, immunity, and pharmacological response...

item.title: Disentangling the gut microbiome and inflammation in inflammatory bowel diseases: longitudinal observations from the IBSEN III study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Despite the well-established involvement of the gut microbiome in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), less is known about how the gut microbiome changes over time and how it varies with clinical dise...

item.title: Epithelial barrier DUOX2 serves as early immune defense in intestinal pathogen control.

item.title: The interplay between probiotics and mast cells in gut inflammation: a mini-review.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - Review - Mast cells (MCs) are myeloid-derived immune cells that differentiate in peripheral tissues, where they are strategically located near epithelial surfaces. In the gastrointestinal tract, MCs regulat...

item.title: Functional changes in the gut microbiota are associated with the intestinal phenotype in A20 haploinsufficiency.
item.description: Pediatric allergy and immunology : official publication of the European Society of Pediatric Allergy and Immunology - A20 haploinsufficiency (HA20) is an autoinflammatory disease driven by pathogenic variants in TNFAIP3, which plays a crucial role in regulating immune responses. item.title: [Overview of the relevance of the intestinal mucus layer of the intestinal barrier - current state of knowledge and clinical perspective].
item.description: MMW Fortschritte der Medizin - Review - The intestinal mucus layer constitutes an essential component of the gut barrier and plays a pivotal role in gastrointestinal health. Disruptions of this layer, particularly due to dietary fiber de...

item.title: [Study on the effect of high-intensity interval exercise on ulcerative colitis in mice].

item.title: Helminth Excretory/Secretory Proteins Ameliorate Colitis Through Preservation of Intestinal Homeostasis.
item.description: FASEB journal : official publication of the Federation of American Societies for Experimental Biology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) manifests as a sustained gastrointestinal condition driven by an ongoing dysregulated immunity, defective mucosal barrier, and microbiota dysbiosis. item.title: Indigo Naturalis regulates the gut microbiota to increase SCFAs content and improve ulcerative colitis lesions.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - The global prevalence of ulcerative colitis (UC) is approximately 5 million, and the incidence is on the rise. The gut microbiota (GM) plays a key role in the pathogenesis and progression of UC. St...
